source: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (EEGMMIDB) v1.0.0
citation: Schalk, G., McFarland, D.J., Hinterberger, T., Birbaumer, N., Wolpaw, J.R. BCI2000: A General-Purpose Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) System. IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering 51(6):1034-1043, 2004.
note: Curated set of real human EEG recordings from PhysioNet's EEGMMIDB, re-named to REACT's PHI-free convention. Spans two same-subject sessions (S001 R01/R02 — for Subject Timeline demo), two additional baselines (S004, S007), and one task recording (S010 motor R03). 64-channel BCI2000, ~1-2 minutes each. Additional EDFs are staged in /seed-edfs-archive/ for easy re-addition.

files.task: Routine EEG with motor task (left/right fist)
